reasoning: Query is about shukubo (temple lodging). 89 matches returned, but top results include a Chinese-language inn (中斋旅馆) unrelated to temple stays, a magazine article about 玉照院 in Nagano (useful), and wikidata temples with no lodging info. The one strong hit (玉照院) is buried. No booking details or direct temple lodging listings surface in the visible portion.
missing_data: Actual shukubo names with addresses, booking contacts, example temple stay programs (zazen, shakyo). The Koyasan/Yoshino/Haguro shukubo cluster should appear prominently.
improvement_hint: Add shukubo as a tagged category in the municipal scrape layer; rank temple-lodging results above generic temple Wikidata entries. A dedicated get_shukubo tool or filter would serve this common intent better.

reasoning: Query asks for onsen villages with yukata-walking culture. 166 温泉 matches returned, but top results are temples (Onsen-ji), mountains (Yusengatake, Onsen Fuji) — not hot spring resort towns. Yunotsu Onsen in Shimane with heritage designations is a genuine match but has no description of yukata-walking ambiance. No famous hot spring resort towns (Kinosaki, Ginzan, Yufuin) visible in top results.
missing_data: Kinosaki Onsen, Ginzan Onsen, Gero Onsen town profiles with explicit mention of togaeri (inter-bath walking). Season info and access details absent.
improvement_hint: Add 温泉街 as a tag distinct from 温泉 (water feature) in ranking; promote municipal scrape entries for resort towns over Wikidata mountain/temple entries when query includes experiential framing.

reasoning: French query about whale watching in Japan. Top result is a park whale statue monument in Nagoya, followed by completely off-topic results: Kyoto river tourism, Monet's Garden in Kochi, Okayama food portal. No actual whale watching spots (Ogasawara, Kochi, Okinawa) appear. The keyword クジラ matched on unrelated content. Severe retrieval failure.
missing_data: Ogasawara whale watching season (Jan-Apr for sperm whales), Kochi Tosa cape humpback whale tours, Zamami/Kerama whale watching in Okinawa.
wrong_data: Top results have no connection to whale watching — they are Nagoya park, Kyoto river boat, Kochi garden.
improvement_hint: Add ホエールウォッチング as a synonym expansion for クジラ queries. Build a maritime/nature activity layer that captures tour operators by species and season.

reasoning: Query asks for deep, authentic tea ceremony experiences in Kyoto. Tool used was get_japan_heritage(prefecture=Kyoto), which returned Japan Heritage narrative stories about the Lake Biwa Canal and Tango silk textiles — no connection to tea ceremony (茶道/cha-no-yu). The data is accurate about what it covers but completely misses the intent. A search for 茶道 or get_traditional_arts with keyword would have been far more appropriate.
missing_data: Tea ceremony dojos in Kyoto (Urasenke, Omotesenke school information), authentic practitioner experiences vs tourist performances, seasonal kaiseki context.
improvement_hint: For intent 茶道, route to search_area with q=茶道 or get_traditional_arts with keyword=茶道; get_japan_heritage without a theme filter returns unranked stories that rarely match niche experiential queries.

reasoning: Query wants mountain villages served by infrequent local trains. search_area with q=ローカル returned: a link policy page (About Linking to this Website), a Hokkaido video library, a Tsugaru DMO PDF document, a DMO name entry, and a festival events page. Zero mountain village results. The keyword ローカル failed to retrieve relevant content about local rail lines or mountain villages.
missing_data: Named rural train lines (Iiyama Line, Akechi Railway, Hisatsu Line), specific villages accessible only by infrequent rail, timetable or access tips.
wrong_data: Top result 'About Linking to this Website' is navigation chrome that should not surface in search results.
improvement_hint: Introduce ローカル線 / rural-rail as a tagged cluster; the generic ローカル keyword is too broad and matches nav pages. Filter out nav/link-policy pages from search_area results more aggressively.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional food culture not yet mainstream. get_local_food without filtering dumps 500 of 24523 GI-registered foods, starting with Aomori Cassis, Tajima Beef, Yubari Melon — well-known premium brands, opposite of 'not mainstream yet.' The constraint to surface lesser-known items was ignored. Data is accurate and well-structured but the ranking doesn't serve the intent.
missing_data: Obscure regional fermented foods, lesser-known GI products from small municipalities, framing that distinguishes niche from mainstream.
improvement_hint: Sort by recency of GI registration or add a 'lesser-known' filter (e.g., low web-search volume proxy). When user asks for hidden/unknown items, prefer tail entries over the canonical top-of-list results.

reasoning: Japanese-language version of L4-01. Identical structure and content, just with Japanese text preserved in the name/description fields. Same issues: 500 of 24523 unfiltered GI foods, starting with Aomori Cassis and Tajima Beef — household names, not hidden food culture. The constraint 'まだメジャーじゃない' (not yet major) is completely unaddressed by the unfiltered bulk return.
missing_data: Filtering or ranking logic to surface lesser-known GI products. Prefecture or category diversification to showcase regional variety.
improvement_hint: Same as L4-01: implement a recency-based or popularity-inverse sort. Consider adding a 'obscure' boolean parameter to get_local_food to return tail GI registrations.

reasoning: Query seeks 'forgotten craftsman skills still alive.' get_traditional_arts returns 183 UNESCO Intangible Heritage items starting with Bunraku, Kabuki — extremely famous arts, opposite of 'forgotten.' Later entries (Chakkirako, Dainichido Bugaku, Hayachine Kagura) are genuinely obscure, but they're buried behind the famous ones. Tool choice is appropriate but ranking fails the intent.
missing_data: Non-UNESCO METI traditional craft items from declining industries; context on which arts face practitioner shortages.
improvement_hint: Add a relevance_score for 'endanger degree' or practitioner count. When query signals 'forgotten/dying,' deprioritize UNESCO A-list arts and surface regionally-specific or newer-designated intangible properties.

reasoning: Query asks for Hokusai-esque landscapes. search_area with q=富士 returns Mount Fuji (prominence 1.0), Fuji Five Lakes, Fujisan Hongu shrine, Fuji Omuro Sengen Shrine, Fujiya Hotel, and the UNESCO property 'Fujisan, sacred place and source of artistic inspiration' — all highly relevant. Heritage designations (Natural Monument, Historic Site, UNESCO) and kinds fields are well-populated. Fuji as the canonical Hokusai subject is directly covered.
missing_data: Other Hokusai-style landscapes beyond Fuji: Kanagawa Great Wave viewpoints, Edo-era bridge scenes in other prefectures.
improvement_hint: Expand query routing to include 北斋 / ukiyo-e landscape synonyms to retrieve non-Fuji scenic spots (sea, bridges, waterfalls) that also match Hokusai's 36 Views.

reasoning: Query asks for 'lesser-known UNESCO sites in Japan.' Tool used was get_japan_heritage, which returns Japan Heritage (日本遺産) — a domestic Agency for Cultural Affairs designation, NOT UNESCO World Heritage or UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage. These are different programs. The 104 stories returned are Japan Heritage narratives, not UNESCO sites. The intent 'lesser-known UNESCO' is not served by this tool.
missing_data: Actual lesser-known UNESCO World Heritage sites: Iwami Ginzan, Hiraizumi, Minamikyushu volcanic arc. Or lesser-known UNESCO Intangible: Chakkirako, Dainichido Bugaku.
wrong_data: get_japan_heritage conflates Japan Heritage (文化庁) with UNESCO. The user asked for UNESCO specifically.
improvement_hint: Clearly distinguish get_japan_heritage (domestic) from UNESCO WHS and UNESCO ICH in tool selection logic. When query mentions UNESCO, route to get_traditional_arts (which has UNESCO category) or a dedicated UNESCO WHS tool.

reasoning: Query asks about areas where Jomon-era culture influences daily life. search_area with q=縄文 returns relevant results: Jomon Goddess artifact (Yamagata, National Treasure), museum in Niigata, Sannai-Maruyama site museum in Aomori, Komakino preservation museum. A Nagano editorial about Jomon experiences with 黒曜石 sites adds depth. Coverage is decent for Jomon museums and sites, though 'influences daily life' framing is not well-addressed.
missing_data: Living Jomon-influenced communities, Tohoku traditions traced to Jomon, festivals with Jomon roots. The 'daily life influence' angle requires editorial content beyond site listings.
improvement_hint: Add a municipal editorial layer for Tohoku/Nagano Jomon heritage communities. Tags for 縄文体験 programs would help agents construct an answer about living traditions.

reasoning: Chinese-language query about fermented food culture across Japan. get_local_food(lang=zh) returns GI-registered foods in Chinese — translation works well. But no filtering for fermented foods (発酵): first items are Aomori Cassis, Tajima Beef, Yubari Melon, none fermented. The specific constraint '发酵食品' (fermented foods) is ignored entirely. Same bulk-dump issue as L4-01/02.
missing_data: Actual fermented food GI products: Ishikawa ishiri (fermented squid sauce), Akita shottsuru, Kyoto nuka-zuke pickles, Aichi hatcho miso, Niigata narezushi.
improvement_hint: Add keyword filtering within get_local_food to match product categories. A get_local_food(q='発酵') filter would dramatically improve this use case.

reasoning: Query about shamanic mountain village traditions. get_traditional_arts returns identical results to L4-03: Bunraku, Kabuki, Chakkirako — performing arts, not mountain/shamanic content. Hayachine Kagura (Iwate mountain shrine dance) is present but buried. The query needed search_area with q=修験道 or q=山岳信仰, or get_japan_heritage with theme filter. Wrong tool selection leads to complete intent mismatch.
missing_data: Itako shamans of Osorezan (Aomori), En-no-Gyoja mountain ascetic sites, Dewa Sanzan shugendo, Togakushi mountain faith communities.
improvement_hint: Route 山岳信仰/修験道/シャーマン queries to search_area with appropriate keywords or get_japan_heritage with theme filter rather than get_traditional_arts which covers performing arts primarily.

reasoning: Query about areas where nuclear bomb history is part of daily landscape. search_area(q=原爆) returns excellent results: Hiroshima Peace Memorial (UNESCO WH, prominence 0.95), Children's Peace Monument, Cenotaph for A-Bomb Victims, Atomic Bomb Memorial Mound, Nagasaki A-bomb Museum. Maruki Gallery in Saitama adds a lesser-known dimension. Heritage designations are well-populated. Hiroshima and Nagasaki are both well-represented.
missing_data: Nagasaki Urakami Cathedral, living survivor community (hibakusha) context, Nagasaki Hypocenter Park. More Nagasaki representation would balance Hiroshima dominance.
improvement_hint: Add a Nagasaki-specific 原爆 cluster to balance Hiroshima results. Consider boosting Nagasaki entries since the query implies 'areas' (plural).

reasoning: Query about towns built around a single craft. get_local_specialty(category=craft) returns 231 METI-designated traditional crafts with production area specifics: Nibutani Attus (Biratori, Hokkaido), Oitama Tsumugi (Yonezawa/Nagai/Shirataka), Ugo Shina Cloth (Tsuruoka/Murakami). Each entry has production_area_text linking craft to specific towns. High-quality structured data with English descriptions. Very useful for agent composition.
missing_data: No map coordinates for craft production towns; tourist workshop/visit info would strengthen practical usability.
improvement_hint: Link craft production_area_text entries to search_area spot data for the same municipality to surface visitor experience options alongside product listings.

reasoning: Query about Japan's industrial decline preservation. search_area(q=産業遺産) returns Tomioka Silk Mill (UNESCO WH, prominence 0.9) as top hit, followed by Nagoya industrial tourism DMO page listing Toyota/Noritake museum sites, Gunma DMO describing Meiji industrial heritage, and a Minamikyushu DMO referencing the Meiji Japan Industrial Revolution WHS in northern Kyushu. Diverse geographic coverage and strong relevance.
missing_data: Hashima (Battleship Island), Iwami Ginzan, Kamaishi iron works — more specific industrial ruin examples.
improvement_hint: Add 軍艦島 / 端島 as a synonym cluster for 産業遺産 searches to surface the iconic Nagasaki industrial ruin.

reasoning: Korean query about Japan's religious diversity. get_japan_heritage(lang=ko) returns all 104 Japan Heritage stories in Korean — translation quality appears good. First result is Asuka era women story (mentions Buddhism+Shinto syncretism), second is Kamakura (mentions Zen temples, Tsurugaoka Hachimangu). Religious diversity content is incidentally present but not the focus. Tool returned all stories without relevance ranking for 종교적 다양성 (religious diversity).
missing_data: Nagasaki hidden Christian heritage, Shugendo mountain syncretism, Okinawa Ryukyuan religion coexisting with Buddhism/Shinto, Ainu spiritual traditions.
improvement_hint: Add theme-based filtering to get_japan_heritage for 宗教 theme. For Korean religious diversity intent, search_area or get_japan_heritage with q=宗教 would surface more targeted content.

reasoning: Query about lost/dying festivals in remote Japan. get_festivals(lang=en) returns 312 Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property festivals — exactly the right corpus. Results include Kibune Matsuri (Manazuru, Kanagawa), Okuma Kabuto Festival (Nanao, Ishikawa), Tatemon Festival (Uozu, Toyama), Yasurai Festival. All are officially recognized traditional festivals, many genuinely remote. Designation label is explicit. High-quality structured data with EN translation.
missing_data: Dates are mostly null, making practical trip planning difficult. No explicit information about which festivals are at risk of discontinuation.
improvement_hint: Add a 'discontinuation_risk' field or flag festivals with fewer than N practitioners. Populate date fields from shrine/temple official calendars. This would turn a good response into an excellent planning tool.

reasoning: Query about coastal areas shaped by 2011 tsunami. search_area(q=震災) returns Namie Town Ukedo Elementary School remains (Fukushima), Sendai symposium, Kamaishi DMC disaster prevention program, Futaba Nuclear Disaster Museum, Iwate TSUNAMI Memorial (Rikuzentakata), Kesennuma disaster memorial, Minamisanriku tsunami park, Tohoku KIZUKI travel program. Comprehensive coverage across Fukushima/Miyagi/Iwate with both wikidata and DMO sources.
missing_data: Yamada Town memorial park, Onagawa town recovery story. Coordinates missing for some entries.
improvement_hint: Add coordinates to DMO designation entries where available to enable geographic routing for coast itinerary planning.

reasoning: Query about Meiji-era pseudo-Western architecture (擬洋風). search_area(q=擬洋風) returned 73 matches but visible results are: Fukui tourism page in Traditional Chinese, Miyazaki tourism homepage in Simplified Chinese, Yamagata tourism in Traditional Chinese, Saga tourism page. All are foreign-language tourism portal homepages with no connection to 擬洋風 architecture. Complete retrieval failure — 擬洋風 matched Chinese-language pages incidentally.
missing_data: Kaichi School (Matsumoto), Meiji Mura open-air museum, Hokkaido Former Government Building, Otaru Bank buildings — canonical 擬洋風 architecture examples.
wrong_data: Chinese-language tourism portal pages appearing as 擬洋風 results is a critical false positive from the scraper.
improvement_hint: Sanitize Chinese/Traditional-Chinese tourism homepage pages from being retrieved by Japanese architectural term queries. Add 擬洋風 as a tagged architectural style in the Wikidata layer.

reasoning: Query about Japan's hidden Christian heritage. get_japan_heritage(prefecture=Nagasaki) returns 8 stories, starting with the Sugar Road (Nagasaki Kaido) — rich food culture content but no direct connection to hidden Christians (隠れキリシタン). The Urakami/Goto Islands hidden Christian heritage exists as a UNESCO WH (2018), but is not in Japan Heritage (日本遺産) corpus. Wrong corpus for this specific query; tool choice constrained the result.
missing_data: UNESCO WH 'Hidden Christian Sites in the Nagasaki Region' (2018): Hara Castle, Ono Church, Shitsu Church, Goto Islands churches. These are UNESCO, not Japan Heritage.
improvement_hint: Add the 2018 UNESCO WH 'Hidden Christian Sites' as a tagged cluster in the wikidata/UNESCO layer. The Japan Heritage corpus doesn't cover this — route 隠れキリシタン intent to a UNESCO WHS tool or search_area with q=隠れキリシタン.

reasoning: Query about gardens by lesser-known masters. search_area(q=庭園) returns 81 results, top hits mostly Tokyo: Hamarikyu, Shiba Rikyu, Kiyosumi garden — all famous, well-documented gardens. Manfukuji garden in Shimane and Kokudo garden in Tokyo add variety. Heritage designations are well-populated. However, constraint 'lesser-known garden masters' is not addressed — no attribution to specific garden designers or information about obscure gardens.
missing_data: Lesser-known gardens: Adachi Museum (Masane Adachi), Jiko-in (Kohori Masakazu), Motoori Norinaga house garden. Attribution to garden masters entirely absent.
wrong_data: 牧野記念庭園 has heritage_designations_labels showing 'Q7309389' instead of a human-readable label — this is a data quality bug.
improvement_hint: Add garden designer attribution field to Wikidata entries. Surface non-Tokyo gardens more prominently for 庭園 queries. Fix heritage_designations_labels to always resolve QID to readable text.

reasoning: Query about traditional textile dyeing experiences. get_local_specialty(category=craft) returns 231 items starting with Nibutani Attus weaving (Ainu textile), Oitama Tsumugi (silk weaving), Ugo Shina Cloth (bark fiber weaving) — all weaving/textile but not specifically dyeing (染色). Relevant craft items are present in the dataset but dyeing-specific entries (Kyoto Yuzen, Indigo Awa-ai, Bingata) are buried or absent from visible portion. Experience locations not specified.
missing_data: Kyo-Yuzen dyeing workshops in Kyoto, Awa-ai (indigo) dyeing in Tokushima, Bingata dyeing workshops in Okinawa — dyeing-specific METI crafts.
improvement_hint: Add a keyword filter to get_local_specialty to narrow by industry_category (e.g., 染色 vs 織物). The current category=craft is too broad for dyeing-specific queries.

reasoning: Query about Edo-period post town (宿場) culture remnants. search_area(q=宿場) returns high-quality results: Kiso road Suharajuku/Nojiri editorial with vivid description of mizufune water culture (Nagano), Yanagimachi post town in Ueda with 300-year-old townhouses and accessibility note, followed by a Tokaido cycling guide from Shizuoka DMO. Content is editorial-rich, geographically specific, and actionable. First hit is particularly strong.
missing_data: Magome-Tsumago post towns (most famous Kiso road segment) not visible in top results. Coordinates for some spots absent.
improvement_hint: Ensure Magome and Tsumago appear for 宿場 queries as they are the canonical destinations. Possibly these are present deeper in the 89 results but should be ranked higher.

reasoning: Query asks for Japanese pilgrimages beyond Shikoku 88. get_japan_heritage with no filter returns all 104 stories. First result is Asuka women's story, second is Kamakura — no pilgrim content. The corpus contains pilgrim-relevant stories (Kumano Kodo, Dewa Sanzan, Ise/Kumano pilgrimages) but they are not surfaced in the visible top results. Theme filtering or keyword search would have been required. Complete ranking failure for a specific content type.
missing_data: Kumano Kodo (Japan Heritage story exists), Dewa Sanzan shugendo pilgrimage, Saigoku 33 Kannon pilgrimage, Chichibu 34 Kannon pilgrimage.
improvement_hint: Add theme_filter='巡礼' to get_japan_heritage when query intent is pilgrimage. The corpus has this data — a theme-filtered call or q='巡礼' search would surface it correctly.
